title: QUIC client-side peer early-data disposition work item
summary: Add the peer EncryptedExtensions early-data disposition branch that surfaces accepted versus rejected disposition, discards dormant ZeroRtt packet-protection material on rejection, and keeps public early-data support out of scope.
planned_changes:
- Observe the peer EncryptedExtensions early-data disposition and surface the narrow accepted-versus-rejected branch internally.
- Discard dormant ZeroRtt packet-protection material on rejection while preserving accepted disposition for later traced ownership.
- Route malformed or duplicated `early_data` signaling through the existing fatal or update path rather than widening parser behavior.
- Add focused requirement-home coverage for accepted disposition, rejected disposition, and malformed or duplicate handling.
completion_notes: The managed client/runtime path now observes peer EncryptedExtensions early-data disposition, discards dormant ZeroRtt packet-protection material on rejection, keeps accepted disposition intact, and leaves malformed or duplicated early_data data closed through the fatal/update path.
verification_plan: Run focused requirement-home tests for accepted and rejected disposition observation, malformed and duplicated early_data rejection, the randomized disposition boundary, and the public no-broad-support guard; run the permanent BenchmarkDotNet suite for the rejected-versus-accepted disposition cleanup branch; then run SpecTrace render and validation checks.
out_of_scope: opportunistic refactors unrelated to the early-data disposition branch
